user_prompt: It has five modes: Bluetooth Connecting, Bluetooth Pairing, Wi-Fi, Cloud, NTP-Sync
The mode and a string (name or SSID) is specified at creation.
For the two Bluetooth modes, the main sprite is `sprit_bluetooth_180x150`, for wifi mode, it is `sprit_wifi_180x150`, and this is to be placed at the top center, not covering the top bar
In the bottom left corner, `sprit_hourglass_60_1` `sprit_hourglass_60_2` and `sprit_hourglass_60_3` shall be animated in a loop, one frame every 333 milliseconds.

In the bottom right corner, there is to be a cancel button, using `sprit_cancel_60`, and it is the only element that is clickable, clicking it should fire a callback function that is written in `main.cpp`

If it is in bluetooth pairing mode, then the sprite `sprit_handshake_60` is placed in the bottom center of the view. Otherwise, it draws text between the two bottom graphics, starting at about (64,180), that says "Connecting to:" and then the name or SSID of the device to be connected to.
The main threads are responsible for transitioning out of this view, because it can either be cancelled, errored, or go into another view.
agent_reply: - Five modes: Bluetooth connecting, Bluetooth pairing, Wi-Fi, Cloud, NTP sync.
- Correct main sprite per mode, centered below the top bar.
- 3-frame hourglass animation at 333 ms per frame.
- Pairing mode handshake sprite in the bottom center.
- Non-pairing text: `Connecting to:` plus the provided name/SSID.
I also ran `pio run -e thefly_bringuptest`; build passed successfully.

user_prompt: find me places where we are not using 1.0f
agent_reply: Places currently using non-`1.0f` text scaling:
`kTextSize = 2.0f`, used for duration and caller info.

Worth noting: [FlyGuiText.cpp](C:/GitHubRepos/The-Fly/lib/FlyGui/FlyGuiText.cpp:201) calls `setTextSize(fontSize_)`, so any `FlyGuiText` instance can be scaled indirectly. The top bar date/time uses `1.0f`; the RecordingView text instances are the non-`1.0f` ones I found.

user_prompt: `RecordingView` for the file name, use font 2 size 1
`RecordingView` for the recording duration, use font 4 size 1
`RecordingView` for the caller information, use font 2 size 1
For all informative screen main text, like the waiting screens, error screens, ModalDialog, use font 2 size 1. The text at the bottom is perfect as is
For the `ScrollView` item text, use font 2 size 1
The top view bar is perfect as is
agent_reply: - footer text kept on its previous font/size and position
`ModalDialog` and the top bar were already aligned with what you asked for, so I left them alone.

commit_message: more gui work, auto bt reconnect

user_prompt: when a `ScrollItem` for a cloud upload destination is triggered, run `CloudUpload::start`

before even that, there should be a callback configured, and it should be configured for a `ModalDialog` with an appropriate icon, thumbs up for success, error for failure. Dismissing the dialog causes the microcontroller to reboot
inside `lib\FlyGuiViews` create a `CloudUploadView` that inherits `ConnWaitingView`, but with a progress update function. It uses the sprite `sprit_cloudupload_100`
during the upload, meaning immediately after `CloudUpload::start` returns true, show the `CloudUploadView`. The cancel button of the `CloudUploadView` causes the `CloudUpload` to cancel.

`CloudUploadView` needs a progress bar, it is 4 pixels below the logo sprite, it is 300 pixels wide and 12 pixels tall, centered. To draw it, draw the frame borders as H-lines and V-lines, then draw a filled rectangle with a color (use HSV where hue for red is 0% progress and hue for green is 50% progress, and not let it exceed the hue for green) with a width representing percentage within the 300 pixels, then the rest is also filled with a black rectangle.

4 pixels below that, use text to say the percentage (do not use decimal places), and then how many files out of total files have been uploaded, include how many have failed, it might look like "12% 5/10 (5 errs)". Use a normal font centered on the screen, but if it gets too long for the screen width then shrink to the smallest font and start from x=1.
the progress drawing should be a part of `updateHourglass`
percentage is bytes transfered vs total bytes
agent_reply: Implemented the cloud upload flow and progress view.
- Cloud upload scroll items now call `CloudUpload::start`.
- The completion callback is configured before starting upload.
- Successful upload shows a thumbs-up `ModalDialog`; failure/cancel shows warning/error. Dismissing the dialog reboots the device.
- Progress bar and progress text update through `updateHourglass`.
- Progress color moves HSV red to green, capped at green.
- Initial `pio run -e thefly_sec0` compiled but hit IRAM0 overflow.
- Sterile rebuild succeeded and produced `firmware.bin`, `firmware.elf`, and `firmware.map`.
